name: Will Rogers
abstract: William Penn Adair Rogers (November 4, 1879 – August 15, 1935) was an American vaudeville performer, actor, and humorous social commentator. He was born as a citizen of the Cherokee Nation, in the Indian Territory, now part of Oklahoma, and is known as "Oklahoma's Favorite Son".

concerts.label: New York Building Superintendents Association Minstrel/Vaudeville Program

about_quotes.speaker: Charles McPherson
about_quotes.evidence: So therefore this is what I mean. You have to be a little bit of a wordsmith. ... you have to have the Will Rogers approach, it’s this really simple language
about_quotes.doc_title: Charles McPherson interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, September 9, 2024 [interview 2 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Jacques Butler
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewee narrates a story about Will Rogers making a picture and grousing about not having a job, but does not claim to have met or worked with him personally.
